description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21 has the full design; eli5.html at the repo root is the design doc; fz-rh2.21.1 (waiting extends) must be landed first. This ticket installs rules 1 and 3: CLAIM SHAPES and ASCENTS WAKE / GROUND SHIFTS REBASE. It is the narrowing/invalidation half of the convergence design — within an epoch evidence only ascends (fz-rh2.21.4 enforces the join); this ticket defines how evidence legitimately comes DOWN when the ground truth changes (source edits), without enshrining any pass structure in the engine and without losing today's minimal-rerun incrementality.

THE CLASSIFICATION. Add FactKey::is_cumulative(&self) -> bool in src/compiler2/drive.rs: true for ReturnType(_) and ActivationInputs(_), false for every other variant. Meaning: a cumulative fact's content is a monotone join maintained by its store (fz-rh2.21.4); a replacing fact's content overwrites. This is a declaration of content algebra (which merge a fact's value supports), not a tier/pass taxonomy — it orders nothing.

THE PROPAGATION RULE. In Scheduler::complete (src/compiler2/scheduler.rs), classify each FactChange before waking dependents:
- ASCENT: first appearance (old_revision None -> Some), or content change (Some -> Some) on a CUMULATIVE key published by a NON-REBASED job. Handling: exactly today's wake (enqueue Current+Settled subscribers and waiters, mark their outputs dirty via the existing mark_dirty call).
- GROUND SHIFT: any retraction (Some -> None); any content change on a REPLACING key; any content change published by a REBASED job. Handling: for every reader (subscribers and waiters of both FactUse::Current and FactUse::Settled forms of the key): mark the reader's claimed outputs dirty (existing mark_dirty), set the reader's REBASE flag, enqueue it. Claims are NOT retracted — they stand, dirty, until the reader re-concludes.
- Readiness-only changes: unchanged (settled-subscriber wake).

THE REBASE FLAG. Scheduler gains rebased: HashSet<J>. Set by ground-shift handling; queried by the drive loop before running a job; cleared when the job CONCLUDES (wait-free completion — a job that waits while rebased stays rebased). Plumbing: the drive loop (World::drive_until in src/compiler2/drive.rs) passes rebased: bool into jobs::run (src/compiler2/jobs/mod.rs adds the parameter; only analyze_activation consumes it, threading to the two World defines below — every other job ignores it) and into World::complete_job (which forwards it to the contribution store).

REBASE SEMANTICS AT THE STORES (the part that makes narrowing sound without cone explosion):
- ActivationMap::define_return (src/compiler2/semantic.rs): non-rebased -> join (installed properly in fz-rh2.21.4; until then last-write is acceptable for this commit); rebased -> REPLACE the stored value with the freshly computed one and reset the ascent counter.
- ActivationInputMap (src/compiler2/semantic.rs) via World::replace_activation_input_contributions: per-publisher entries JOIN on non-rebased completions (refine_widen of old and new entry — today they replace; change it) and REPLACE on rebased completions. Cross-publisher join (already refine_widen) unchanged. On waiting completions (per fz-rh2.21.1): union/join, never remove.

WHY LAZY (no eager transitive retraction): if a shift eagerly retracted the whole transitive read cone, redefining a helper whose recomputed return is IDENTICAL would still reset every transitive caller. With rebase, the helper re-runs in replace-mode, publishes changed=false, and the cone stops at depth one — exactly today's propagation behavior, now with join/replace correctness. The cone reaches exactly the jobs whose recomputed outputs actually differ.

WHERE EPOCHS COME FROM: an exogenous edit enters as a replacing-fact content change (e.g. re-submitted code -> CodeIndexed/FunctionSource content change), which is a ground shift by the rule above, which rebases readers, whose real changes shift THEIR readers, etc. No epoch object exists; "epoch" is the quiet between ground shifts in a job's read cone.

TESTS (TDD):
- scheduler_test.rs: (a) content change on a replacing fact rebases+enqueues readers and dirties their claims without retracting them; (b) content change on a cumulative fact from a non-rebased publisher wakes readers WITHOUT setting rebase; (c) retraction always shifts; (d) a rebased job that publishes changed=false propagates nothing and clears its rebase; (e) a rebased job that waits stays rebased.
- world_test.rs: compiler2_activation_inputs_retract_one_publishers_stale_contribution currently pins per-publisher contribution REPLACEMENT narrowing the join mid-flight — under this design that is exactly the defect-4 churn. Rewrite it: narrowing a contribution now requires the publisher to be REBASED (simulate: complete the publisher job with the rebased path) and the joined value then narrows; a non-rebased re-completion with a different contribution must JOIN (widen), not narrow.
- semantic.rs test activation_return_replaces_stale_broad_result_with_current_precise_result pins within-epoch descent (define broad, then precise, expects precise). Rewrite: descent requires rebase; without rebase the values join.
- drive_test.rs redefinition suite must stay green as-is — these pin the whole point: compiler2_redefining_main_retracts_the_old_root_frontier_and_activates_foo, compiler2_redefining_uncalled_foo_does_not_reopen_quicksort_root, the helper-redefinition minimal-rerun tests near them. If any fails, the propagation classification is wrong — do not adjust the test.

Acceptance: green per epic baseline, zero warnings, one commit. The quicksort runaway is NOT expected to be fixed by this ticket alone (the monotone join lands in fz-rh2.21.4).
title: fact engine: claim shapes; ascents wake, ground shifts rebase
